stories.title: Isa Torres Commits to Texas — D1 POY Says "I'm Coming Home"
stories.angle: D1 Softball Player of the Year Isa Torres ended a 10-day wait on June 16 (Tuesday evening), committing to Texas and posting "I'm coming home" on Instagram in a burnt orange photo. Torres, a native of Georgetown, TX — the same hometown as Longhorns catcher commit Samantha Swan — is heading home to the two-time national champions. She joins Swan (.383 BA / 1.151 OPS / 12 HR / 55 RBI, No. 1 catcher in the portal) and Samantha Lincoln (former Texas Tech pitcher) in Texas's portal class. Mike White's Longhorns now hold three elite transfers headlined by the best player in college softball.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
SHE'S GOING HOME.

Isa Torres — D1 Player of the Year, .530 BA, .978 SLG — commits to Texas.

"I'm coming home." The Georgetown, TX native is headed to Austin to join the two-time national champions.

The portal just gave Texas its crown jewel. #HookEm #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Texas's 2027 portal class:

✅ Isa Torres — D1 POY, .530 BA, .591 OBP
✅ Samantha Swan — No. 1 catcher, .383 BA, 12 HR
✅ Samantha Lincoln — pitcher (ex-Texas Tech)

Mike White already had two titles. He just stacked the roster again.

#HookEm #SoftballTwitter

article.headline: Isa Torres Commits to Texas — D1 Player of the Year Says "I'm Coming Home"

stories.title: Beachum Watch — Day 10, Five Days Left and Still Uncommitted
stories.angle: With Torres off the board at Texas, Jaysoni Beachum (.417 BA / 16 HR / 66 RBI / .549 OBP) is now the most coveted uncommitted transfer in the portal. The Softball America No. 1 transfer and 2026 Second-Team All-American (career: 37 HR / 180 RBI / 202 hits) enters Day 10 of the 15-day window with five days remaining. Texas, Florida, and other Power programs are believed to be in the mix. Torres's decision to choose Texas may or may not influence Beachum's deliberations, but the clock is now her primary adversary.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Torres is gone. Texas A&M and everyone else just turned their full attention to Jaysoni Beachum.

Softball America's No. 1 transfer. .417 BA. 16 HR. 66 RBI. Career 37 HR, 180 RBI.

Day 10. Five days left. The portal's last big name. #SoftballTwitter

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Jaysoni Beachum is still deciding.

2024 NFCA Freshman of the Year.
2026 Second-Team All-American.
Career: 37 HR, 180 RBI, 202 hits at FSU.

She watched Torres go to Texas last night. What does Beachum do? 5 days left. #SoftballTwitter

article.headline: Beachum Watch — Day 10, Five Days Left, Last Elite Transfer Still Uncommitted

stories.title: Texas's Portal Masterpiece — Torres + Swan + Lincoln = Three-Peat Blueprint
stories.angle: Texas head coach Mike White has assembled one of the most formidable portal classes in the history of the sport. Torres (D1 POY, .530/.978/.591, 78 runs) fills the shortstop role with an all-time offensive performer. Swan (.383 BA / 1.151 OPS / 12 HR / 55 RBI) is the No. 1 catcher in the portal. Lincoln (former Texas Tech pitcher) adds pitching depth. The Longhorns are the two-time defending national champions. They just got better.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Two national titles. Three elite portal adds.

Texas's path to a three-peat runs through:

→ Isa Torres (D1 POY)
→ Samantha Swan (No. 1 catcher)
→ Samantha Lincoln (pitcher)

No. 1 is hard to beat once. Who stops them three times? #HookEm #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Texas's Portal Masterpiece — Torres, Swan, and Lincoln Build the Three-Peat Blueprint

stories.title: Texas Tech's Class Nearly Complete — Polar + Heavener + Pickering DNT
stories.angle: Texas Tech's 2026 transfer class — No. 1 pitcher Jayden Heavener, nation's top hitter Moriah Polar (.555 BA / 101 hits), and Kasidi Pickering (.370 BA / 20 HR / 61 RBI, DNT tag) — is within one announcement of being complete. Pickering has yet to officially confirm, but the DNT tag and all reporting point to Lubbock. If it holds, Jerry Glasco's Red Raiders, who fell to Texas in the WCWS championship game, will have assembled the class needed to return the favor.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Texas Tech's portal class, nearly complete:

✅ Polar — .555 BA, nation's top hitter
✅ Heavener — No. 1 pitcher in the portal
🔜 Pickering — .370 BA, 20 HR, DNT → Lubbock

Texas vs. Texas Tech 2027 just got very real. #WreckEm #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Texas Tech's Transfer Class Is Nearly Complete — Pickering's DNT Tag Is the Last Piece

stories.title: Portal Countdown — Day 10 of 15, Five Days Remain
stories.angle: Day 10 of the 15-day transfer portal window. Five days remain before June 22 close. Committed: Swan (Texas), Torres (Texas), Lincoln (Texas), Plumlee (Alabama), Altmeyer (Texas), Azua (Texas A&M), Hartley (OSU), Wetteland (Arkansas), Heavener (Texas Tech), Polar (Texas Tech). DNT/expected: Pickering (Texas Tech). Still available: Beachum (No. 1 remaining, Softball America). The two biggest programs in college softball — Texas and Texas Tech — have staked their claims. The last major card belongs to Beachum.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Transfer portal: Day 10 of 15. Five days left.

✅ Torres → Texas
✅ Polar → Texas Tech
Still out there: Beachum (Softball America No. 1)

The two biggest programs are loaded. Who lands Beachum? #CollegeSoftball

article.headline: Transfer Portal Countdown — Day 10 of 15, Five Days Remain
